question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the order parameter causes getOrderByQuery() to produce an ORDER BY clause with both token_id and serial_number in DESC order, and by triggering the three-subquery UNION ALL path cause the DB to perform three independent DESC sorts before the outer merge sort, consuming sort buffer memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getSubQuery() to produce a WHERE clause where the spender IN clause contains values that are all NULL or zero, causing the DB to perform a full table scan to evaluate the IN predicate, and by sending many such requests simultaneously exhaust DB I/O on 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the three-subquery UNION ALL path and supply a large limit value, causing each subquery to return up to limit rows before the outer UNION ALL merge, so that the DB must process up to 3*limit rows in memory before applying the outer limit, amplifying memory usage and degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/nftService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNft()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent getNft() requests with tokenId values that map to tokens with very large numbers of NFTs, causing the DB index scan on (token_id, serial_number) to traverse many index pages, exhausting DB I/O bandwidth shared by 30%+ of processing nodes?
